calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DANIEL RAY BACON, ESQ., LAW OFFICES OF DANIEL BACON, 415.864.0907, FOR PLAINTIFF JERRY LANKFORD.
LISA BERKOWITZ, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar. Duplicate entry. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DANIEL RAY BACON, ESQ., LAW OFFICES OF DANIEL BACON, 415.864.0907, FOR PLAINTIFF JERRY LANKFORD.
LISA BERKOWITZ, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Plaintiff alleged eight causes of action. Plaintiff's first and second causes of action fail because he has not shown that he suffered an adverse employment action. Plaintiff's first cause of action also fails because he has not presented any admissible evidence that the City's legitimate, nondiscriminatory reason for placing him on administrative leave was a pretext for race discrimination. Plaintiff's second cause of action also fails because he has not presented any admissible evidence that the City's legitimate, nondiscriminatory reason for placing him on administrative leave was a pretext for retaliation against him for engaging in a protected activity. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit to his third, fifth and seventh causes of action. Plaintiff's fourth and eighth causes of action are barred due to plaintiff's failure to comply with the Government Tort Claims Act. Plaintiff's sixth cause of action was dismissed by the federal court. San Francisco Police Department has demonstrated legitimate, nondiscriminatory reasons for the administrative leave without pay. In turn, Mr. Lankford has not presented admissible evidence giving rise to an inference of unlawful discrimination. COUNSEL SUBMITS PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-06-2012 Continued To Sep-25-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter.
RULING: The Court vacated its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT or in the alternative summary adjudication is continued to september 25, 2012 at 9:30 am in department 302. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-18-2011 Off Calendar
